title: Thai-flagged cargo ship struck by missiles in Strait of Hormuz, 20 rescued
content: As the West Asia war escalates, a Thai-flagged cargo vessel was struck by missiles in the volatile Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday (March 11). According to a report in the Bangkok Post, which cited the Royal Thai Navy, 20 crew members were rescued by the Royal Navy of Oman. But there is no information on the three crew members, who were on board. Mayuree Naree, the bulk carrier owned by the Thai company Precious Shipping Pcl, had left Khalifa Port in the UAE and was moving towards Kandla Port in India. It was struck by missiles while transiting the Strait of Hormuz.Iran has claimed responsibility for the attack. According to news agency PTI, Thailand's acting deputy prime minister and Transport Minister Phiphat Ratchakitprakarn, shared initial information that showed the vessel had left a UAE port and was approximately 20 kilometers offshore when the attack took place.

title: Crystal Lake family-owned diner Andy’s marks 45 years in business Thursday
content: Some things never change. For the cozy Crystal Lake staple Andy’s Restaurant, that means always having homemade soup, homemade gravy and a good cup of coffee. The family-owned diner, located at 468 W. Virginia St., is celebrating its 45th anniversary on Thursday with 45-cent coffee and other beverages, along with a special anniversary cake. But for the family that keeps the restaurant running every day, it’s a milestone that honors the founder’s legacy. Owner Angie Angelos looks lovingly of the multiple photos of her dad framed on the walls of the diner. When her father, Andrew Piculas, moved to Crystal Lake, he bought Lakeview Restaurant on March 12, 1981, and renamed it Andy’s. She has been working there ever since. Piculas came to America through Ellis Island from Greece. One of his first jobs was dishwashing, where he learned to cook by watching over the chef’s shoulder. From there, he owned a diner called Stephans Restaurant in Chicago, Angelos said. She describes her father as having had a “heart of gold,” an infectious smile and a giving nature. Angelos took over as the owner with her two brothers after Piculas died at the age of 92 in 2022. Angelo Angelos, Angie’s son, works as the manager of the restaurant. He takes great pride in his position, knowing his grandfather created a space many consider a pillar in the community. “It’s important to keep the family’s legacy,” he said. One way the family keeps Piculas’ legacy alive is by continuing small traditions he started, like handing out coins and free suckers to children. “I’m definitely my father’s daughter,” Angie said. She attributes their long-running success in the restaurant business to making and keeping relationships with customers. Many longtime customers have brought in multiple generations of their families over the years. “It makes me so happy to see it,” she said. That longterm loyalty is also seen in the staff. The restaurant currently has a team of about 20 employees, many who have been there for years. Cook Jorge Guzman has worked in the kitchen for over 40 years, and server Mary Kotecki has been connecting with diners for almost 30 years. Another key to success: always have “a good cup of coffee, a cup of homemade soup and homemade gravy,” Angie said. The family has faced its fair share of challenges over the 45 years in business. Recent years have been particularly hard, like dealing with soaring egg prices and the COVID-19 pandemic lockdown . Most recently, Angie’s brother...
creator: michelle meyer

title: Caldmore Primary pupils help design bins to cut fly-tipping
content: Caldmore Primary pupils help design bins to cut fly-tipping A project trialling raised litter bins has significantly reduced fly-tipping in a hotspot location. Three wall-mounted bins were installed in October 2025 around St Michael Street car park, an alleyway near Caldmore Primary Academy and community gardens, and Caldmore Green. Permission was secured from Caldmore Community Garden and Caldmore Primary Academy to mount the bins on their fence posts. The results over the past four and a half months have been encouraging: St Michael Street car park: 1 reported fly-tip since installation, compared to 16 in the previous 12 months. Carless Street (near the school and community gardens): 0 reported fly-tips since installation, compared to 11 in the previous 12 months. Caldmore Green alleyway: 1 reported fly-tip since installation, compared to 6 in the previous 12 months. The project also included a strong community engagement element. Pupils at Caldmore Primary Academy were invited to design labels for the front of the new bins, helping to build a sense of pride and ownership in the area. Three winning designs, created by Jessika Kasbia, Jennifer Kasbia and Magda Webster have now been installed on the bins. Councillor Suky Samra, Portfolio Holder for Street Scene at Walsall Council, said: “This is a fantastic example of how innovation, data and community involvement can come together to make a real difference. The reduction in fly-tipping in these locations shows that simple, well-thought-out changes can have a significant impact. “I want to thank Caldmore Primary Academy and the local community for getting behind the initiative. By involving young people and creating a sense of ownership, we are not only improving the environment now but helping to shape attitudes for the future." The council is now considering whether this approach could be expanded more widely across the borough, particularly in areas where litter bins are repeatedly misused as informal waste collection points. The raised bin initiative forms part of Walsall Council’s wider commitment to reducing litter and fly-tipping, supporting cleaner, safer neighbourhoods and encouraging community pride.
creator: walsall council

title: Higher smartphone prices are on the horizon due to RAM costs
content: Smartphone prices in 2026 are going to increase as prices surge for RAM, according to a new report from Counterpoint Research. This is an outcome that was probably inevitable. While companies could eat the cost to avoid raising prices on consumers, that isn’t really a viable option when the goal is to make money as a business. In addition to that, increased RAM prices have already led to higher prices for several other types of products. More expensive GPUs, higher prices on RAM kits, laptops and desktops, you name it. All of these have gone up in price because the RAM shortage has left manufacturers with fewer options and constricted supply, and that has made the supply that’s left cost a little more. As the bill of materials goes up for manufacturers, those same manufacturers are bumping up the price on the products that those materials are for. With regard to smartphones, we’ve already seen this happen on some devices. The Samsung Galaxy S26 and the Samsung Galaxy S26 Plus, for example, both go on sale today, and they cost a little more compared to last year’s Galaxy S25 series. The only one that didn’t go up in price was the Galaxy S26 Ultra. Samsung has not confirmed that the higher RAM prices are the reason for these price increases, but it is the most logical explanation. As Samsung’s phone prices have gone up, other brands are sure to follow. Smartphone prices in 2026 are expected to increase by as much as $200 It’s worth noting that this isn’t a guaranteed figure for phones, and it all depends on not only the manufacturer but also the category of the phone. Counterpoint’s findings expect that low-end phones could go up in price by about $30. High-end flagships, meanwhile, could see prices that go up by as much as $150 to $200. As noted, this has already happened with the Galaxy S26 series. The Galaxy S26 is $40 more this year, while the Galaxy S26 Plus has gone up by $100 compared to last year. Counterpoint isn’t guaranteeing that prices are going to rise, but it does note that it seems unavoidable. Brands will want to recoup the money that was necessary to acquire more expensive materials. The easiest way to do that is to pass the cost to the consumer. This could also lead to decreased sales of smartphones in 2026. Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra – $1,299.99 Buy from The post Higher smartphone prices are on the horizon due to RAM costs appeared first on Android Headlines .

title: U.S. Military Admits Devastating Mistake in Iranian School Bombing, 175+ Killed
content: BitcoinWorld U.S. Military Admits Devastating Mistake in Iranian School Bombing, 175+ Killed In a sobering admission with profound international implications, the U.S. military has confirmed a catastrophic targeting error led to the bombing of an Iranian elementary school, resulting in at least 175 fatalities, most of them children. This devastating revelation, reported by Walter Bloomberg and stemming from a preliminary CENTCOM investigation, marks a significant moment in U.S.-Iran relations and modern military accountability. The February 28th strike, executed with Tomahawk missiles, was reportedly based on flawed intelligence from the Defense Intelligence Agency (DIA) that failed to identify the building’s current civilian use. U.S. Military Mistaken Bombing: The Investigation’s Findings The preliminary investigation by U.S. Central Command (CENTCOM) has concluded that the February 28th strike constituted a “targeting error.” Consequently, the attack originated from intelligence provided by the Defense Intelligence Agency (DIA) that was critically outdated. The targeted structure in Iran’s Sistan and Baluchestan province was indeed formerly associated with a naval installation. However, it had been repurposed and functioning as the Seyed al-Shohada Elementary School for over three years prior to the attack. Military analysts note that such intelligence failures, while rare, highlight persistent challenges in dynamic conflict zones where infrastructure use can change rapidly. The strike involved multiple Tomahawk Land Attack Missiles (TLAMs), precision-guided weapons known for their accuracy, which makes the intelligence lapse particularly consequential. Timeline and Context of the Iranian School Attack Understanding the event requires examining the sequence of actions and statements. The strike occurred in the early hours of February 28th. Initially, regional news outlets and the Iranian government reported a direct hit on a civilian school. The U.S. Department of Defense did not immediately confirm the operation. In the following days, President Donald Trump publicly dismissed the reports as a potential “hoax” by Iranian authorities. This stance shifted dramatically with the completion of the preliminary military investigation. CENTCOM formally briefed the National Security Council on March 15th, leading to the public admission. The timeline underscores the procedural gap between military action, internal review, and public accountability. Expert...

title: Shaun Cassidy performs at Foxwoods
content: If you were a preteen or teen girl in the mid to late 1970s, you remember Shaun Cassidy — the poppy hit singles, the Teen Beat covers, the "Hardy Boys" series. Cassidy them morphed from a teen idol into a behind-the-TV-scenes success. He created, wrote and produced shows like "American Gothic," "Cold Case" and "Cover Me." He was an executive producer and writer for five years on "New Amsterdam." Now, he is circling back to music — and touring. He brings his "The Road to Us" tour to Foxwoods on Friday. He talks about his life and his famous family, and he sings some of those charting tunes from way back when, including "Da Doo Ron Ron," "That's Rock 'n' Roll" and "Hey Deanie." Shaun Cassidy, 8 p.m. Friday, Great Cedar Showroom, Foxwoods; $39 and up; foxwoods.com.

title: Musical Masterworks present 'The Sacred and the Profane'
content: Now, here's a provocative title for a classical music performance: "The Sacred and the Profane." That's the name for the concert the vaunted Musical Masterworks series will present this weekend in Old Lyme. According to Masterworks, the program "traces a compelling arc across centuries, exploring the tension between devotion and desire, ritual and rebellion, light and darkness." The musicians will be violinist Tessa Lark, who is Masterworks artistic director, harpist Bridget Kibbey, violinist Chad Hoopes, violist Matthew Lipman and cellist Mihai Marica. The compositions are Debussy's Danses sacrée et profane, Bach's Toccata and Fugue in D minor in an arrangement for solo harp, Britten's Lachrymae, John Dowland's lament Flow My Tears, and Kibbey's Bards and Griots, as well as works by Henriette Renié and Heinrich Ignaz Franz von Biber. It all leads up to the final number, André Caplet's The Mask of the Red Death, inspired by Edgar Allan Poe. And get there early: the musicians will give pre-concert talks 45 minutes before each show. Musical Masterworks, 5 p.m. Sat. and 3 p.m. Sun., First Congregational Church in Old Lyme, 4 Lyme St., Old Lyme; $45, $5 for students; musicalmasterworks.org.
creator:
by kristina dorsey

title: Richins trial live: Attorneys prepare for prosecutors' final witness in Park City murder trial
content: Jeff O’Driscoll, the lead detective in the case against Kouri Richins, who is accused of murdering her husband, will testify at 1 p.m. on Wednesday at the Park City courthouse. O’Driscoll, a detective with the Summit County Sheriff’s Office, was expected to begin his testimony on Monday, but multiple motions attorneys needed to resolve caused delays. Third District Judge Richard Mrazik granted a one-day continuance on Tuesday to give Richins’ attorneys time to review transcripts from interviews between officers and witness Carmen Lauber. The attorneys agreed to prepare a written motion for the portions they would like to be admitted into evidence. Kouri Richins, 35, is charged with aggravated murder and attempted aggravated murder, first-degree felonies, two counts of insurance fraud, a second-degree felony, and forgery, a third-degree felony. She also has a separate case with additional financial charges, which will not be considered by this jury. She is accused of fatally poisoning her husband, Eric Richins, on March 4, 2022, and of attempting to poison him weeks earlier. Wednesday will be the 12th day of her jury trial, which is scheduled to last over a month. This story will be updated.

title: Germany and Japan to partially release 'strategic' oil reserves
content: Germany and Japan to partially release 'strategic' oil reserves Germany will release part of its oil reserves as part of an internationally coordinated effort to mitigate price increases on international markets stemming from the Iran war, Economy Minister Katherina Reiche told reporters on Wednesday, AzerNEWS reports. "The International Energy Agency yesterday evening asked its member states to release oil reserves of 400 million barrels. We will accede to this request and make our contribution," Reiche said in Berlin. Earlier, Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi said that her country would release oil reserves as early as next week to ease pressure on the prices of petrol and other energy sources. Spain approved the move as well. Earlier Monday, French President Emmanuel Macron, whose country holds the rotating presidency of the Group of Seven advanced economies, said the G7 would this week discuss a possible release of strategic oil reserves. A possible meeting of G7 leaders on the energy issue could take place this week, Macron told journalists on his way to Cyprus. However, speaking to reporters after chairing a video meeting of G7 finance ministers, French Finance Minister Roland Lescure said the group was "not there yet". Asked if they had agreed on releasing stockpiles to lower prices, Lescure said from Brussels: "What we've agreed upon is to use any necessary tools, if need be, to stabilise the market, including the potential release of necessary stockpiles." Such a measure can only be effective if it is implemented in a "coordinated" manner, Lescure added. The war triggered by US-Israeli strikes on Iran is fuelling fears for the global economy, with global stock markets sinking and oil rocketing above $100 a barrel for the first time since Russia's invasion of Ukraine in 2022. title: Modern Micro Endodontics Opens New Parsippany, NJ Location to Expand Access to Advanced Endodontic Care
content: PARSIPPANY, N.J., March 11, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Modern Micro Endodontics is pleased to announce the opening of its newest office location in Parsippany, New Jersey. This expansion reflects the practice's continued commitment to providing advanced, patient-focused endodontic care to communities throughout Northern New Jersey. Modern Micro Endodontics is a multi-location specialty practice dedicated exclusively to endodontic treatment, including root canal therapy and the management of complex dental conditions. Known for combining cutting-edge technology with compassionate care, the practice focuses on preserving natural teeth while prioritizing patient comfort and clinical precision. With offices throughout New Jersey, Modern Micro Endodontics has built a reputation as a trusted referral partner for general dentists and specialists alike. The practice utilizes advanced diagnostic and treatment tools, including dental microscopes and 3D imaging, to deliver predictable, high-quality outcomes in both routine and complex endodontic cases. "We are excited to bring Modern Micro Endodontics to Parsippany and continue expanding access to specialized endodontic care," said Dr. Vivian Graham. "This new location allows us to better serve patients and referring dentists in the area while maintaining the same high standards of technology, precision, and compassionate care that define our practice." Modern Micro Endodontics provides a comprehensive range of endodontic procedures and services, including: - Root canal therapy - Endodontic retreatment - Surgical endodontics (apicoectomy) - Treatment of cracked or fractured teeth - Traumatic dental injury care - Emergency endodontic services - Advanced diagnostic imaging using CBCT technology About Modern Micro Endodontics Modern Micro Endodontics is a leading New Jersey endodontic specialty practice dedicated to delivering advanced, patient-centered care using state-of-the-art technology. The practice focuses exclusively on endodontics, offering precise diagnosis and treatment designed to relieve pain, preserve natural teeth, and support long-term oral health. Modern Micro Endodontics welcomes referrals and patients seeking specialized endodontic care at its Parsippany location, located at 245 Baldwin Rd, Suite 207, Parsippany, NJ 07054, and can be reached at 973-335-3700 or online at https://www.jcendo.com. title: Three held over attempted abduction of woman in Moulvibazar's Kamalganj
content: Police have arrested three men over an attempted abduction of a woman using a private car in Moulvibazar's Kamalganj upazila and seized the vehicle used in the incident. Confirming the matter, Officer-in-Charge Md Abdul Awal said, the suspects were arrested from different areas of Sylhet yesterday (10 March), police said today (11 March). The arrestees are Md Zakir Mia, 26, son of Motchir Ali from Sripur (Nasirabad) village in Kulaura upazila; Md Kawshar Ahmed, 34, son of Abdur Rahman from Saiyarpur area of Moulvibazar Sadar upazila; and Jasim Mia, 36, son of Nur Mia from Gulbagh (Berirchar) area of the same upazila. The incident took place on 8 March, and CCTV footage of the attempted abduction later spread online. According to police and the victim's account, the woman from South Baligaon village under Ward No. 5 union of Kamalganj was waiting at Bottola Bazar around 6:30am to travel to Srimangal for work. At that time, a white private car approached her and offered to take her to Srimangal. Although she declined, the vehicle continued to move around the area and later followed her. At one stage, a man inside the car tried to forcibly pull her into the vehicle by covering her mouth. The woman managed to escape after a struggle. Following the complaint, a case was filed with Kamalganj Police Station under Sections 7/30 of the Women and Children Repression Prevention Act, 2000. After the investigation, the police conducted raids in several areas of Sylhet city and arrested the three suspects. Based on information provided by the arrestees, the private car used in the attempted abduction was recovered from inside the Agra Community Centre in the Sobhanighat area. OC said the suspects were detained during drives in areas including Bandar Bazar and Sobhanighat in Sylhet. Legal action has been taken against the accused, and the investigation into the case is ongoing, he added.
creator: the business standard

title: Paris-Nice: Jonas Vingegaard climbs into race lead with massive effort on stage 4 marked by high-speed chaos and grueling weather conditions
content: You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful Paris-Nice was thrown into chaos and the overall turned upside down on Wednesday, as a late high-speed mass crash forced GC leader Juan Ayuso (Lidl-Trek) and other top riders to abandon injured, whilst Jonas Vingegaard (Visma-Lease a Bike) captured the victory and the yellow jersey in the race's first summit finish overall. On the last kilometre of the category 1 ascent to Uchon, Vingegaard blasted away from the tiny front group of three who had managed to avoid the major crash that left Ayuso out of the race, finishing nearly a minute ahead of Red Bull-Bora-Hansgrohe leader Dani Martínez, his teammate Tim Van Dijke claimed third. On a bitterly cold, rainy day through the hills of central France where for many riders the only objective came down to just being able to finish, the effects of an early echelon of 40 riders containing Ayuso, Vingegaard and Martínez already saw the race shattered into pieces long before the final climbs and the mass crash. Article continues belowIneos Grenadiers co-leader Kévin Vauquelin, previously third overall, was amongst those dropped early, while teammate Oscar Onley suffered after an earlier fall and then a mechanical and finished more than three minutes down. However, it was the big pile-up at 55 kilometres on a sweeping descent that upended the race the most, seeing Ayuso fall heavily and abandon, whilst other top contenders badly affected included Brandon McNulty (UAE Team Emirates-XRG), who abandoned, and Spanish National Iván Romeo (Movistar). Initially with four riders in a front group of five, Red Bull were able to push on with Vingegaard, who finally soared away unanswered by Martinez one kilometre from the rain-soaked summit to claim the victory and the lead. How it unfolded In the opening segment of stage 4, a flat run east out of Bourges, there had been plenty of predictions about echelons, and they proved 100% correct. No sooner had the starting pistol sounded than Ayuso, Vingegaard and Onley all made it into an elite front group of 40. Amongst those top names missing the cut from the leaders were French climbing star Lenny Martínez (Bahrain Victorious), Onley's teammate Kévin Vauquelin and Visma-Lease a Bike's Bruno Armirail. Ayuso, on the other hand, had two teammates to work for him, Jakob Söderqvist and Mathias Vacek, Vingegaard was supported by Eduardo Affini, and Onley had no less than three fellow Ineos Grenadiers racers, Michael...
creator: alasdair fotheringham

title: Ex-Deliveroo driver charged with sexual assault of young girl in Dublin
content: Ex-Deliveroo driver charged with sexual assault of young girl in Dublin The accused appeared in court on Wednesday after being arrested on Tuesday in relation to the alleged incident on February 15. A former Deliveroo driver has appeared in court charged with the sexual assault of an eight-year-old girl in north Dublin. The man, aged 27, cannot be named due to court restrictions on sexual assault cases in Ireland. He appeared before Judge Michele Finan on Wednesday after being arrested on Tuesday in relation to the alleged incident on February 15. Garda Grainne Raggett told Dublin District Court that the accused denies the charge. She said Deliveroo had provided dates and timestamps for his location on the day of the incident. She said a search warrant was executed on Tuesday and the accused’s Deliveroo jacket and bag, waterproof trousers and mobile phone were seized. His passport and driving licence were also seized. The garda said the accused, who followed court proceedings through an interpreter, came to Ireland through the UK two years ago, and has no valid immigration status in Ireland. She said he left Deliveroo employment around two weeks ago. She also said gardai were investigating two other alleged incidents and that further charges may be brought. Ciara Murray BL, representing the defendant, said “he said he did not do what he is alleged to have done”, and although he was in Ireland illegally, he had worked for two years and had “made his life here in Ireland”. Ms Justice Finan said Garda Raggett had made a “very comprehensive and cogent” argument and refused bail. The accused was granted legal aid and remanded in custody at Cloverhill before his next court appearance on March 18.
creator: gráinne ní aodha

title: Doubts raised over bill allowing courts to deport foreign criminals
content: Concerns were raised on Wednesday by legal authorities and international organisations over a proposal to allow courts to order the deportation of foreign nationals convicted of serious crimes. The proposal, submitted by Disy MP Nicos Tornaritis, was discussed at the House legal affairs committee, where officials warned that the measure could conflict with existing procedures and raise legal and human rights complications. The bill seeks to introduce judicial deportation as an additional penalty that courts could impose on foreign nationals from third countries who are convicted of serious offences . Supporters say the measure would align Cyprus with other European states that allow deportation following criminal convictions. Tornaritis told the committee that the measure was intended to strengthen public safety and mirror practices already applied elsewhere in Europe. However, legal service representatives warned that Cyprus already has an administrative system capable of deporting individuals who pose a threat to public order. They raised concerns that introducing a court-ordered deportation penalty could blur the line between criminal sentencing and immigration procedures. A legal service representative said the existing administrative mechanism allows authorities to act quickly when foreigners are deemed dangerous, describing it as “quite effective”. The official questioned whether a criminal court would have access to the full information necessary to make deportation decisions without risking breaches of international obligations. Particular concern was expressed in regard to avoiding refoulement , which prevents the return of individuals to countries where they may face persecution or danger. Officials also questioned how courts would handle cases in which a convicted individual subsequently applies for asylum. Speaking on behalf of the deputy migration ministry, Maria Adamidou expressed her reservation on whether deportation should be introduced as a criminal penalty. She argued that such an approach could raise issues of equal treatment within the legal system and recommended that the proposed law avoid listing specific offences because doing so could prove restrictive. The debate also drew attention to the UN refugees commission, which stressed that any deportation framework must clearly safeguard fundamental rights. A UN representative cautioned the committee that the legislation should explicitly reference the non-refoulement principle,...
creator: james morphakis

Eufy Omni C28 robot vacuum review: flagship features with a £150 saving in the spring sale (aff)
content: Eufy Omni C28 robot vacuum review: flagship features with a £150 saving in the spring sale (aff) This article contains affiliate links. We may earn a small commission on items purchased through this article, but that does not affect our editorial judgement. Eufy is a company that's always had a habit of taking top-tier tech and making it affordable. And its latest launch is no exception. For a while now, flagship robot vacuums have been sporting some mildly effective rotating mop heads, but times they are a-changing. And the rotating mop is now all the rage. The trouble has been that flagship robot vacuums have quite often come in at a four-figure sum, which rules out normal people with normal budgets. And this is where Eufy steps in. This new C28 has a roller, but costs £649. And that's only if you miss a special offer, which brings the price down to £499. It's mid-range money for a flagship performer, then, right? Sort of. It comes extremely close, in fact. It has 15,000Pa of suction, which is very impressive. It has a five-in-one base station that empties waste, collects waste water, and washes and dries the mop. It even has a clever detangling brush that prevents pet hair from clogging, and two rotating brushes to sweep in dust as it vacuums. The only limitations are fairly superficial beyond that, and completely forgivable given the price of the robot. Firstly, the rotating mop is brilliant. It's forced on to the surface with 1kg of pressure, and it's being constantly self-cleaned from an on-board clean water and dirty water tank system. It also has a scraper, to ensure there aren't any chunks sticking to it. All well and good, then, but unfortunately it doesn't extend like some seriously expensive robot vacuums do. This means that it can miss edges and tight spaces - although the sort-of-square shape of the vacuum does compensate somewhat. It also has a trick obstacle avoidance system, and it's great for avoiding toys and random bits of clutter, but it's less effective with smaller stuff. And if you're house-training a puppy, that could be a nightmare. There are lots of other things to like, though. It's got quite a compact base unit, for a robot vacuum with so many features. It's fairly quiet, and Eufy's app is one of the best in the business. There's even a mulit-floor setting for people who have time to carry their robot up a flight of stairs. I've lived with plenty of Eufy robot vacuums, including some of the flagships, and they all impress me...
creator: gareth butterfield

title: MKBHD explains why MacBook Neo is “better than you think”
content: Apple’s new MacBook Neo may look like a basic entry-level laptop on paper, but tech YouTuber Marques Brownlee says it performs better than many people might expect. Brownlee, known online as MKBHD, shared his impressions of the $599 MacBook Neo after using the laptop for a week. The device runs Apple’s A18 Pro chip, which also powers the iPhone 16 Pro lineup, and is positioned as the company’s most affordable Mac laptop in years. In his video review, Brownlee said the laptop exceeded his expectations once he began using it in real-world scenarios. “But it turns out this Neo is better than you think. Even better than I expected.” MKBHD breaks down MacBook Neo verdicts by use case Brownlee said the MacBook Neo delivers performance similar to Apple’s original M1 chip in several benchmarks, including Geekbench and Cinebench. According to his testing, the A18 Pro inside the laptop can handle everyday tasks like web browsing, spreadsheets, email, and word processing without issue. He added that the laptop’s performance is especially strong when using Apple’s native apps, with single-core performance allowing programs to open quickly and remain responsive during multitasking. https://youtu.be/iGeXGdYE7UE?si=Do8fMgpq5HWJrFMu To help viewers understand where the laptop fits best, Brownlee created a series of ratings for different types of users. For students, Brownlee gave the MacBook Neo an A+, calling it an ideal option for schoolwork and everyday tasks. College students received an A rating, with Brownlee noting that the laptop should handle most coursework unless a major requires more demanding software. Writers also received a strong recommendation. Brownlee said the laptop is well-suited for word processing, browsing, and email, adding that it uses the same keyboard switches found in Apple’s higher-end MacBook Pro models. Photographers were given a B- rating. Brownlee said the Neo can handle photo editing software such as Pixelmator and Lightroom, though he noted the display does not cover the full P3 color space, meaning professionals may want to connect an external monitor. For coders, Brownlee assigned a B rating. He said the system is capable for basic development work but the 8GB of memory may limit heavier workloads. Video editing received a lower score. Brownlee demonstrated that Final Cut Pro can run basic 4K edits on the machine, but said more demanding workflows with plugins quickly push the system to its limits. Podcasting was rated B+, with...
creator: dylan horetski

title: Budget which has '£2m improvements for Gosport services' but hike to tax approved
content: Gosport Borough Council has approved a £15.2 million budget for 2026/27, with council tax set to rise 2.99 per cent. For a Band B property, council tax will increase to £1,823.33 a year, which the administration says equates to less than 12p extra per week. The budget was passed by 14 votes to 12 at a full council meeting on March 4, after the Liberal Democrat administration secured support from Labour councillors. However, despite presenting what it described as a balanced budget for the coming year, the authority faces growing financial pressures in the years ahead. Council forecasts show a projected deficit of around £1.5 million in 2026/27, with the budget relying on £1.36 million from the Revenue Financing Reserve to balance the books. Looking further ahead, the council expects a budget gap of £3.3 million by 2029/30. To close that gap, the authority will need to find at least £1.5 million in savings in 2027/28, another £1.5 million in 2028/29, and around £300,000 in 2029/30. Speaking at the meeting, council leader Cllr Peter Chegwyn (Liberal Democrat) defended the budget, saying it protects services while keeping tax rises relatively low. Cllr Chegwyn said: “This budget is good for Gosport because we are only increasing council tax by less than inflation. “The difference between us and others is that we are not cutting services. This budget includes around £2 million of improvements to services in Gosport, including more support for the voluntary sector, play areas and facilities across the borough." Cllr Chegwyn said the administration had managed to deliver a balanced budget despite inheriting a £1.7 million deficit from the previous Conservative administration. “We’ve worked hard to produce a balanced budget that protects services and keeps council tax down, but it hasn’t been easy.” While acknowledging ongoing financial pressure, Cllr Chegwyn defended the use of reserves. “We are taking money from reserves to help balanced the budget, is what reserves are there for, there are for rainy days.” He highlighted investment in leisure and recreation, including £1.8 million in new sports facilities, improvements at the leisure centre, resurfaced tennis courts and new outdoor facilities along the seafront. The council is also supporting regeneration and heritage projects, including the restoration of the Criterion Cinema. Cllr Chegwyn said the project is being funded through external grants and developer contributions rather than council tax, telling...
creator: natalia forero

Person rushed to hospital after incident outside busy central London station
content: A person has been rushed to hospital after an incident outside Fenchurch Street station. The incident was reported in Fenchurch Street just before 1.35pm today (March 11). Officers and London Ambulance Service crews were called to the scene. An ambulance crew, an incident response officer and paramedics from the hazardous area response team also attended. The person was treated at the scene and taken to hospital where their condition is unknown. The nature of the incident is unclear at this stage. Fenchurch Street remains open and trains are running normally, according to C2c on X. We have contacted the London Fire Brigade for further information. A spokesperson for London Ambulance Service said: “We were called at 1.34pm today (11 March) to reports of an incident on Fenchurch Street, EC3M. “We sent resources to the scene including an ambulance crew, an incident response officer and paramedics from our hazardous area response team (HART). “We treated a patient at the scene and took them to hospital as a priority.”
creator: berk uyal

title: Free bus travel will help make life ‘cheaper, easier and better for everyone’ in Scotland
content: Free bus travel in Scotland will be ‘transformative’ says MSP as Scottish Greens announce bold new policy The Scottish Greens have unveiled plans to introduce free bus travel for everyone in Scotland, the party has announced. The scheme would be funded by taxing the super-rich and big corporations. The party hope that if buses are the most affordable transport in Moray, it will significantly reduce the number of cars by allowing people to catch the bus, making roads safer, less congested and result in less pollution caused by traffic. Scottish Greens MSP for the Highlands and Islands, Ariane Burgess said: “We want to make life cheaper, easier and better for everyone in Scotland, and our vision shows that both people and planet can benefit. “Our policies so far prove the work can be done. “Every young person in Scotland can now travel by bus for free - and no-one in the Highlands and Islands will pay more than £2 per journey from March [23] - because of us. These hundreds of millions of journeys have saved money for young people and their families, and it has been transformative so far. “Across the Highlands and Islands, as a result of Scottish Green budget negotiations, from March 23 no-one will pay more than £2 for a single bus fare. Fares such as a single adult journey from Wick to Inverness, currently £31, and a single adult journey from Elgin to Inverness, currently £15.60, are now just £2, leaving residents, commuters and visitors with more money to spend in local businesses. These great deals should encourage more people to use their local buses. “Free bus travel has opened up opportunities to many who wouldn’t have had the chance otherwise. Jobs, university and college courses, exploring more of Scotland, seeing family and friends regularly. “Why wouldn’t you want to offer more people the same opportunity? I certainly believe that everyone in Moray is deserving of that. That is why we have committed to delivering free bus travel for everyone in Scotland in our manifesto. “This can be a reality by taxing the wealth of the super-rich and big corporations to pay for it. We can achieve all of this on a bus network brought back into public control, ending four decades of failure from privatisation. “The people of Scotland deserve a bus network that works for them, keeps money in their pockets, and doesn’t cost the earth either. The Scottish Greens are promising to deliver just that.”
creator: alan beresford

title: JERAT, Slavko (Steve)
content: It is with profound sadness that the family of Slavko (Steve) Jerat announce his passing on March 4, 2026, at the age of 78. He passed away peacefully, surrounded by loved ones, at the Sturgeon Community Hospital in St. Albert, Alberta following a long but courageous battle with dementia. He was a devoted husband, father, grandfather, and friend. Slavko will be deeply missed by his family, many friends, and all who had the privilege of knowing him. A Prayer Service will be held at Connelly-McKinley St. Albert Funeral Home, 9 Muir Drive, St. Albert, AB T8N 1G1, on March 17, 2026 at 7:00 p.m. A Funeral Mass will be held at the St. Albert Catholic Parish, 7 St Vital Ave, St. Albert, AB T8N 1K1 on March 18, 2026 at 10:30 a.m. To send condolences, please visit www.connelly-mckinley.com Connelly-McKinley Funeral Home St. Albert Chapel (780) 458-2222

Mabaruma gets new garbage compactor truck
content: See full statement from the Regional Democratic Council of Region One: The Regional Democratic Council of Barima-Waini (Region One) and the Municipality of Mabaruma extend sincere gratitude to His Excellency Dr. Mohamed Irfaan Ali, the Ministry of Local Government and Regional Development, and by extension the Government of Guyana, for the timely intervention in providing a garbage compactor truck to serve the Town of Mabaruma. For many years, garbage collection and disposal within the municipality relied largely on tractors to service the growing residential and business community of Mabaruma. The addition of this modern compactor truck will significantly improve the efficiency of waste collection, enhance sanitation services, and contribute to a cleaner and healthier environment for residents. The truck was officially delivered to the Municipality of Mabaruma on Tuesday March 10, 2026 marking an important step forward in strengthening municipal services and improving the quality of life for citizens. The Regional Democratic Council and the Municipality once again express heartfelt appreciation to the Government of Guyana for this important investment in the continued development and transformation of our communities.
creator: inews

title: ICE operation in South Burlington leads to multiple crashes, protest
content: SOUTH BURLINGTON, Vt. (WCAX) - An Immigration and Customs Enforcement operation in South Burlington on Tuesday morning led to multiple crashes, a foot pursuit, and a crowd of protesters, according to South Burlington Police. South Burlington Police say they responded to a 911 call just after 7:30 a.m., reporting a multi-vehicle crash near 345 Dorset St. They say officers found four damaged vehicles in the area that appeared to be used by ICE agents as they were trying to box in a suspect in a parking lot, who ended up crashing into them. The suspect then fled on foot. Police say ICE later requested assistance from officers to monitor protesters who had formed in the area as ICE agents surrounded a nearby apartment looking for the suspect. This is a developing story. Copyright 2026 WCAX. All rights reserved.
creator: wcax news team

title: Suspected criminal damage incident reported in Aylesbury
content: On November 23 at around 7am, it is believed that a vehicle parked on Upende was scratched causing £250 worth of damage. Thames Valley Police was told that the damage occurred on the car’s door panel. A spokesperson for the police force added: “We believe there may be more victims, if you have any information relating to these incidents, please call 101 and quote the reference 43250607929.” Editor’s note: A CCTV appeal connected to this incident has ended. A person Thames Valley Police wanted to speak to in connection with the suspected damage was identified.

title: Major update on controversial expansion of Hampshire recycling site as hundreds hit out at plans
content: Planning officers at Hampshire County Council say the application fails to demonstrate a special or local need for development on a greenfield site, which lies in designated countryside outside the settlement boundary. The proposal, submitted by Silverlake Garage, would expand and reorganise the existing site. Plans include a dismantling building, offices, a research and development facility and a building for removing batteries from electric vehicles. Several existing buildings would be demolished, and a new access road and staff parking area would also be created. In a report to councillors, officers said that the development’s layout, scale, mass and design, along with associated lighting and fencing, would appear as an “incongruous and intrusive feature within the countryside”. They conclude it would cause “unacceptable visual harm” and have a “significant adverse impact on the character and appearance of the surrounding rural landscape”. The report also raises concerns about the impact on the setting of Row Ash House, a Grade II listed building located next to the site in Shedfield. According to planners, the proposed buildings — including a 14.5-metre-high dismantling building and a five-metre-high acoustic fence close to the boundary — would erode the agricultural setting that contributes to the historic character of the property. While the impact on the listed building is described as “less than substantial”, officers say it is not justified by wider public benefits. Campaigners from Community Against Silverlake Expansion (CASE), a residents’ group formed by people living in and around Shedfield and the surrounding area, have strongly opposed the scheme. Speaking on behalf of the group, Simon Barker said the council’s report showed there were “multiple reasons” the expansion should not go ahead. Mr Barker said: “Hampshire County Council’s report is clear that there are multiple reasons why Silverlake’s proposed expansion is inappropriate. “The industrialisation of the field into one of Europe’s largest single-site car recycling factories would cause permanent damage to the rural character of the area. “The 14.5-metre-high main building — with a footprint larger than one and a half international football pitches — together with a minimum five-metre-high acoustic wall, constant movements of heavy goods vehicles, and lighting across the field during winter evenings would be an outrageous intrusion. “The proposed expansion would destroy the setting of...

title: الدفاعات الجوية في طهران تتصدى لأهداف معادية في سماء العاصمة
Oyo Police Arrest Three Armed Robbery Suspects, Recover Stolen Items
content: Following its intelligence-led policing and proactive stop-and-search operations, men of the Oyo State Police Command have arrested three suspects who had been on its watchlist for armed robbery, recovering a series of stolen items and a gun. According to the State Police Public Relations Officer, Ayanlade Olayinka, “operatives on patrol at the General Gas Area, Akobo, Ibadan, intercepted a suspect riding a motorcycle. The suspect, later identified as Abiodun Adeyemi Michael, attempted to flee on sighting the operatives but was swiftly pursued and apprehended. Investigations have revealed that the suspect had been on the Police radar for some time and is known to operate along the state borders of Lagos, Ogun, and Oyo, engaging in armed robbery activities”. The arrest and search conducted on the suspect led to the recovery of the many items including: One Magnum Pump Action gun with six live cartridges; Mobile phones: iPhone 17, iPhone 15, iPhone 13, iPhone 8, iPhone XR, iPhone 16 Blue, iPhone 14 Pro Max, Tecno Camon, Tecno CKG, Tecno Spark 20, Tecno CM6, and five units of Redmi phones (black); One Samsung Galaxy S10; One Wi-Fi device; One power bank; One Oraimo radio; Four wristwatches, and cash sum of ₦130,000′′. During interrogation, the suspect allegedly “confessed to participating in a robbery operation alongside three accomplices, namely Olaoluwa Pamole, Ahmed Ismail, and Lamidi, during which unsuspecting members of the public were robbed at gunpoint around the Toll Gate and Egbeda areas of Ibadan. Acting on this information, a team of police operatives, in collaboration with men of the Command Monitoring Unit and led by the Divisional Police Officer, Akobo Division, proceeded to the suspects’ meeting point at Access Hotel, Sawmill, Onipepeye Area, Iwo Road, Ibadan. At the scene, Olaoluwa Pamole and Ahmed Ismail were arrested, while the third suspect, Lamidi, is currently at large. “The suspects further confessed to being involved in the snatching of a Honda car, ash in colour, in Abeokuta, Ogun State. The Commissioner of Police, Oyo State Police Command, CP Femi Haruna, has directed that the case be transferred to the Command Monitoring Unit for discreet investigation and prosecution. He has also ordered that the fleeing suspect be apprehended and prosecuted to face the full weight of the law”, it was said. Members of the public were therefore urged to remain vigilant and continue to promptly report suspicious persons or activities to the nearest...
creator: new telegraph

title: Sioux Falls man killed in ejection crash on I-29
content: HARRISBURG, S.D. — A Sioux Falls man has died following a rollover crash on Interstate 29 near Harrisburg. Shortly after 11:30 a.m. Tuesday, March 10, first responders in Lincoln County were called to the mile marker 71 of Interstate 29, the Tea-Harrisburg exit, for a report of a single-vehicle rollover crash. ADVERTISEMENT The South Dakota Highway Patrol said a 47-year-old Sioux Falls man was operating a GMC Denali northbound on Interstate 29 near the Tea-Harrisburg exit when the vehicle gradually traveled off the roadway and entered the median. The vehicle vaulted over a culvert and began to roll, causing the driver to be ejected. The driver was taken to a Sioux Falls hospital where he was pronounced dead. He was not wearing a seat belt at the time of the crash, troopers say. The name of the victim has not yet been released pending family notification. The South Dakota Highway Patrol continues to investigate the crash.
creator: hunter dunteman

title: Inmate appears in court charged with murder of Soham killer Ian Huntley
content: An inmate has appeared in court charged with the prison murder of Soham killer Ian Huntley. Anthony Russell, 43, appeared by video-link for a five-minute hearing at Newton Aycliffe Magistrates’ Court, County Durham, on Wednesday afternoon. Wearing a grey sweatshirt and sitting at a table, he spoke only to confirm his name and that he understood that he will appear at Newcastle Crown Court on Thursday. Russell is charged with a single offence of murdering the 52-year-old. District Judge Steven Hood told him: “The offence of murder can only be heard in the crown court. Therefore I am sending this matter to Newcastle Crown Court and you will appear at Newcastle Crown Court tomorrow morning. “The reason for that is because in matters such as murder, the magistrates’ court cannot consider anything other than a remand in custody, and the law states that a person must appear within 48 hours before a crown court.” Huntley was reportedly attacked with a metal bar in a workshop at maximum security HMP Frankland on February 26. He was taken to the Royal Victoria Infirmary, Newcastle, where he died on Saturday morning. The ex-school caretaker was serving a life sentence for the 2002 murders of 10-year-olds Holly Wells and Jessica Chapman. Huntley killed the best friends after they left a family barbecue to buy sweets in Soham, Cambridgeshire, on August 4 2002. He dumped their bodies in a ditch 10 miles away. They were not found for 13 days, despite a search involving hundreds of police officers. At the time, Huntley lived with Maxine Carr who was a teaching assistant at Holly and Jessica’s primary school. He denied murdering the girls but was convicted after a trial at the Old Bailey in 2003. He was jailed for life with a recommended minimum term of 40 years. Carr gave Huntley a false alibi and was jailed for 21 months for perverting the course of justice. She is now living under a new identity.
creator: pa news agency

title: Landrum approves business license ordinance, hears update from Farmers Market
content: Landrum approves business license ordinance, hears update from Farmers Market Published 11:48 am Wednesday, March 11, 2026 LANDRUM—Landrum City Council approved the second reading of an ordinance updating the city’s business license structure during its regular meeting on Tuesday, March 10, at Landrum City Hall. Ordinance No. 2026-01 amends the city’s business license ordinance to update the class schedule required under the South Carolina Act 176 of 2020. The ordinance also revises the city’s business license rate schedule, removes declining rates from the schedule and adds new verification requirements for gross receipts. Council held a public hearing on the ordinance prior to the vote. No public comments were made during the hearing. City staff said the update brings Landrum’s rates more in line with other municipalities in the region and reflects rising costs associated with providing city services. Officials also noted that it has been more than a decade since the city last adjusted its business license rates and that the current fiscal year budget anticipated additional revenue from the changes. Following the public hearing, the council unanimously approved the ordinance on second reading. City Administrator Shannon Baldwin also presented a brief overview of projects and initiatives completed over the past year. Among the efforts highlighted were the development of the city’s first strategic plan and planning tied to downtown improvements and the future Saluda Grade Trail. Baldwin also said a recent mapping effort revealed the city maintains about 20 miles of streets—eight more miles than previously documented. The meeting marked Baldwin’s final meeting as city administrator. During the meeting, Landrum Police Chief Levi Bouwkamp reported that the department is working to modernize its evidence management system ahead of an audit by the South Carolina Law Enforcement Division. Officers are currently digitizing handwritten evidence records to create a searchable database. Bouwkamp also highlighted the effectiveness of the city’s Flock license plate reader cameras. The system recently helped identify a vehicle involved in a hit-and-run that damaged a gas pump at a local business and assisted another agency in locating a burglary suspect who had traveled through the area. Additionally, Landrum Farmers Market Director Kristen Johnston presented the market’s annual report. The market celebrated its 25th anniversary in 2025. Johnston introduced the...

title: Sony's new 2025 65-inch BRAVIA OLED TV is back to its lowest price saving you $800
content: Recently we covered a couple of surround sound system deals starting with the 5.1 Logitech Z906 for just $410, followed by the 11.1.4 Dolby Atmos Samsung HW-Q990F for only half its MSRP. If you are already covered in that area and looking to upgrade your display, then Sony's BRAVIA XR8B 65-inch Class OLED 4K HDR Google TV is a very good option at the moment as it is back to its lowest price of just $1200 (purchase link under the specs table down below). Thanks to its 65-inch size, the BRAVIA XR8B can become the centerpiece for both cinema viewing and gaming at your home. The OLED panel Sony says will produce "pure, deeper blacks and dazzling colors, while Sony’s XR AI Processor claims to enhance color, contrast, and clarity by analyzing the different scenes playing on the screen. For gamers, the XR8B is tuned with PlayStation 5 in mind. Features such as Auto HDR Tone Mapping and Auto Genre Picture Mode should optimize visuals automatically, while support for 4K at 120fps and Variable Refresh Rate (VRR) is there to ensure tear-free gameplay. For sound, the TV's Acoustic Center Sync allows the unit to pair up with Sony’s BRAVIA Theatre soundbars, turning the screen itself into part of the audio system, adding to the center channel experience in a surround sound setup. title: Data Center Market to Reach US$ 801.5 Billion by 2033 Expands Amid Digital Transformation and AI Infrastructure Demand - Persistence Market Research
content: LONDON, March 11, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- The global data center market is expanding rapidly, expected to be valued at around US$347.3 billion in 2026 and projected to reach US$801.5 billion by 2033, growing at a CAGR of 12.7% during the forecast period. This growth is driven by accelerating digital transformation, expanding cloud adoption, and increasing demand for high-performance computing infrastructure. Data centers enable the processing and storage of massive volumes of data generated by AI, IoT devices, streaming platforms, and enterprise applications. Market expansion also reflects significant investments in hyperscale and colocation facilities worldwide, with operators focusing on energy efficiency, modular infrastructure, and sustainable power solutions. Key Highlights - The global data center market is projected to grow strongly, reaching US$801.5 billion by 2033 as demand for cloud computing, AI workloads, and digital infrastructure accelerates worldwide. - North America leads the market, accounting for over 39% of market share, supported by hyperscale deployments and strong technology sector presence. - Asia Pacific is the fastest-growing region, expanding at a CAGR of 18.5%, driven by rapid digital transformation, 5G rollout, and increasing cloud adoption across emerging economies. - Hardware dominates the component segment, capturing over 56% market share with a value exceeding US$ 194.5 Bn, reflecting large investments in servers, GPUs, storage systems, and networking infrastructure. - DCIM software records the fastest growth at 16.7% CAGR, as operators deploy advanced monitoring and automation tools to improve energy efficiency and facility management. - Rising investments in hyperscale and colocation data centers are reshaping the industry, enabling enterprises to scale digital services while reducing infrastructure ownership costs. - Increasing focus on energy efficiency and renewable power integration is driving innovation in cooling technologies, power management systems, and sustainable data center architectures. title: Korean YouTuber Freezia under fire for not wanting her boyfriend to use Galaxy phone
content: YouTuber and influencer Freezia is facing backlash online after saying she wouldn’t like it if her boyfriend used a Samsung Galaxy phone. The comment came during a recent vlog uploaded to her YouTube channel The Freezia, where the Single’s Inferno star chatted with a friend while getting her nails done. During the conversation, the topic turned to smartphones and dating preferences. Freezia, whose real name is Song Ji-a, said she’d “kind of dislike it” if a boyfriend used a Galaxy device. She explained that both of her parents use Samsung phones but felt the situation would be different for a partner. “The thought of him taking my picture with that phone... ugh, so annoying,” the YouTuber with nearly 4.6M followers on Instagram said. Korean YouTuber’s Galaxy phone comments spark backlash She went on to suggest photos taken on Galaxy phones have a different look compared to iPhones and admitted she doesn’t remember ever using a Samsung device herself, only her parents’. “And even if you take a portrait shot the proportions are all wrong. And the whole vibe of Galaxy photos is completely different from an iPhone.” https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jv6DJHEll4U Although Freezia confessed when the Flip came out, she was tempted to buy one for herself. “It reminded me of the old flip phones. But I still think the iPhone is way easier. When I use my mom's phone, it's so difficult.” Clips from the video quickly spread across social media, where viewers accused the influencer of being dismissive toward Galaxy users. Some commenters argued that publicly criticizing a widely used brand, especially in South Korea, where Samsung is a major company, was unnecessary or rude. “It sounds like she's judging people by their phone,” one remarked. “I just think they're prejudiced against people who don't use iPhones,” another chimed in. Others defended Freezia, saying the remark was simply her personal preference about smartphones and dating. The controversy marks another moment of scrutiny for the influencer, who previously faced backlash in 2022 over allegations she wore counterfeit luxury goods during the height of her popularity after appearing on Netflix’s Single’s Inferno. This isn’t the first time a Korean YouTuber’s vlog has sparked discussion across the country. Last year, mukbang influencer Tzuyang went viral for revealing she needed surgery after significantly wearing down her teeth by eating so much.
creator: michael gwilliam

title: Jack White defends comments about Taylor Swift's 'boring' songwriting
content: March 11, 2026 Jack White is defending himself against the Swiftie army. Taking to his Instagram Stories on Monday, March 9 ,the White Stripes frontman clarified his comments about Taylor Swift’s songwriting — comments which went viral hours earlier from his interview with The Guardian. In the interview, White suggested he finds it uninteresting to write songs about personal experiences, referencing the trend of pop stars detailing public breakups in their music, aka, the “Taylor Swift way.” After criticism quickly spread online, the six-time Grammy winner explained that his comments were about his own creative approach rather than a criticism of Swift or other artists. "What I was trying to say in an interview I did about poetry and lyric writing, was that I don’t find it interesting at all for ME to write about MYSELF in my own lyric writing and poetry because I think that it could be repetitive for ME to always write about and It could be uninteresting for people who listen to my music to delve into, and that imaginary characters are more attractive to me as a writer," White wrote on his since-expired Stories, according to Rolling Stone. He emphasised that different artists have their own creative processes and that Swift’s success speaks for itself. "Because I say I have a way of doing things doesn’t mean that I think that EVERYONE should do it the same way," White continued. "They should do what works for them, And they do, and it is obviously appealing to many people, and I’m glad to hear that." White also used the moment to reflect on the pressures of modern media coverage. He said the “age of this massive demand for click bait and content” has made him increasingly hesitant to do interviews, claiming that even a small comment can be pulled out and “spit out as bait.”
creator: geo news digital desk

title: Prof. K.N. Panikkar: Historian, educator and beacon of secular thought
content: The passing away of Professor K.N. Panikkar is a big loss to Indian historiography, pedagogy, and public discourse. Born on 26 April 1936 in Guruvayur, Kerala, he was one of India’s most influential Marxist historians and a steadfast public intellectual. His rigorous scholarship illuminated the intersections of culture, colonialism, ideology, and popular consciousness, while his commitment to secularism and evidence-based inquiry resisted distortions of the nation’s past. Panikkar completed his undergraduate studies in Kerala before earning his Master’s and doctorate from Rajasthan University. It was there that he met and later married his college mate Usha Bhargava (who predeceased him). The couple had two daughters. After establishing himself as an exceptional teacher at Delhi University, Panikkar joined Jawaharlal Nehru University’s Centre for Historical Studies in 1972. Alongside luminaries such as Bipan Chandra, Sabyasachi Bhattacharya, and S. Gopal, he helped build one of the most dynamic departments of modern Indian history in post-independence India. Panikkar was renowned for his pedagogical brilliance, making intricate subjects vivid and accessible. His pioneering course on the 'History of Ideas in 19th-century India' redirected attention from prevailing economic and political emphases toward culture’s pivotal role in shaping human action and large-scale political movements. Panikkar’s work critiqued colonial historiography’s reductive views of indigenous culture, instead highlighting how 19th-century Indian intellectuals forged modern paradigms without Western endorsement. Figures like Akshay Kumar Dutt and Lokahitawadi exemplified an autonomous modernity, while his explorations of traditional systems such as Ayurveda under colonial rule exposed the resilience and complexity of Indian knowledge traditions. .Andre Béteille, 1934–2026: Quiet architect of Indian Sociology. His key publications include British Diplomacy in North India (1968), Culture and Consciousness in Modern India (1970), National and Left Movements in India (1980), and the landmark Against Lord and State: Religion and Peasant Uprisings in Malabar, 1836–1921 (1989). In this influential study, he reframed the Malabar rebellion — often mischaracterised as the Moplah Rebellion — as a layered struggle involving anti-imperialist resistance, agrarian revolt, and elements of freedom fighting. He also edited significant documentary collections, including Peasant Protest and Revolts in...
creator: hasnain naqvi

title: Rustico Mustangs lead Montague Stallions in senior hockey playoff series
content: With the possibility of being pushed to the brink of elimination, the Montague Stallions responded in a big way. The Stallions went into Game 3 of the best-of-seven semifinal series in the P.E.I. Senior Hockey League trailing the Rustico Mustangs 2-0. The Mustangs jumped out to a 2-0 lead in the second period in Game 3, before the Stallions roared back with five unanswered goals to record a 5-2 win. Weather permitting, the Stallions were scheduled to host Game 4 in Montague, P.E.I., on March 11. Montague will also host Game 5 on March 14 at 7:40 p.m. Related The first-place Stallions hosted the series opener, won by the Mustangs 5-3, and are hosting the next two games after Games 2 and 3 were played in North Rustico. The Mustangs won Game 2 by a 6-1 score. Stallions 5 Mustangs 2 Jack Campbell (2-1), Jean-Marc Renaud (1-1), Jack King and Hunter McIntyre scored for the Stallions, who trailed 1-0 after the opening period but answered back to take a 3-2 lead into the second intermission. Cole Jamieson added three assists. Zac MacKay netted both goals for the Mustangs. Overall, the Stallions outshot the Mustangs 27-18. Winning goaltender was Nick Jamieson. Mustangs 6 Stallions 1 Nick Currie (2), MacKay (1-1), Brent Andrews (1-1), Kris MacEwen and Dan Rice scored for the Mustangs. Eddie Jenkins countered for the Stallions. The score was tied 1-1 after the first period and the Mustangs took a 4-1 lead into the second intermission. Matt Jelley earned the goaltending win and Rustico outshot Montague 31-22. Mustangs 5 Stallions 3 The Mustangs received a 54-save performance from Jelley and held period leads of 2-0 and 4-2 en route to a series-opening victory. MacKay (2), Andrews (1-2), Josh McKinnon (1-1) and Currie found the back of the net for Rustico. Jack King (2) and David Ling (1-1) answered for Montague. Overall, the Stallions enjoyed a 57-33 shots-on-goal advantage.
creator: jason simmonds

title: Her får barna servert brødskivene på et kunstverk
This Alberta town just repealed its youth curfew bylaw
content: A bylaw meant to restrict Okotoks youth from being out at all hours of the night was repealed this week, after no records were found of it being used in at least eight years. The youth curfew bylaw had been in place since 1998, but Okotoks council repealed it during their meeting on March 9. A recent review of the bylaw found little reason to keep it, Municipal Enforcement Manager Vik Kulkarni said. "(The bylaw) has demonstrated limited effectiveness in addressing youth-related concerns, presents enforcement challenges, and may unnecessarily restrict lawful activity by youth," Kulkarni said. The bylaw was adopted to keep youth under 16 from being in a public place without supervision or a vaild reason between 12:01 a.m. and 5 a.m., seven days a week. Valid reasons were travelling directly to or from a paid or volunteer job, or working at a paid or volunteer job in a public place. According to the legislation, the bylaw was passed to promote safety and well-being, protect property, and “assist parents and legal guardians with exercising authority over their children to keep them safe from harm.” Youth between 12 and 15 found in violation of the bylaw could have been fined $100. Parents or guardians of youth of any age found violating the bylaw could have been found guilty of “lack of supervision” and fined $100. The Town maintains records going back eight years and found no evidence the bylaw was used in that time. “The fact that we have no records...I think proves the fact that it’s time to repeal this,” Coun. Stefan Dykema said. Kulkarni said modern approaches to dealing with youth-related issues focus on prevention and engagement instead of restrictions. “Youth curfews have raised concerns in many jurisdictions regarding proportionality and potential impacts on youth rights,” he said. Criminal or nuisance matters involving youth can be dealt with under federal or provincial laws or Okotoks' community standards bylaw, he said.
creator: robert korotyszyn

title: AP News Summary at 11:47 a.m. EDT
content: Iran targets ships, Dubai airport and oil facilities as economic concerns mount DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Iran has attacked commercial ships and targeted Dubai International Airport. The latest attacks on Wednesday mark an escalation in its campaign to bottle up the oil-rich Persian Gulf as American and Israeli airstrikes pound the Islamic Republic. Iran’s response to the surprise Israeli and U.S. bombardment 12 days ago has upended trade routes, choked supplies of fuel and fertilizer coming out of the Gulf and threatened air traffic through one of the world’s most-traveled regions. Both sides have dug in, hoping to outlast the other. An Israeli intelligence assessment found that Iran’s new supreme leader was wounded at start of the war. Trump keeps telling America he's winning in Iran. He's less clear in explaining how the war ends WASHINGTON (AP) — When it comes to the war with Iran, President Donald Trump has cycled from calls for “unconditional surrender” to sounding amenable to an end state in which Iran trades one hard-line ayatollah for another. The shifting comments from the Republican president and his top aides have added to the precariousness of the 12-day-old conflict that's affecting the Middle East and causing economic tremors around the globe. With neither side budging, the war is now on an unpredictable path and a credible endgame is unclear. IEA agrees to record release of emergency oil reserves in an effort to calm surging prices PARIS (AP) — The International Energy Agency has agreed to release the largest volume of emergency oil reserves in its history, in a bid to counter the effects on energy markets of the war in the Middle East. The Paris-based organization said Wednesday that it will make 400 million barrels of oil available from its members’ emergency reserves. It’s a larger stock than the 182.7 million barrels that were released in 2022 by the IEA’s 32 member countries in response to Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine. During a videoconference of the Group of Seven leaders Wednesday, French President Emmanuel Macron praised the emergency release, saying it amounts to the equivalent of 20 days of the volume being exported through the Strait of Hormuz. US stocks remain calm even as oil prices keep swinging NEW YORK (AP) — The U.S. stock market is remaining relatively calm, even as the price of oil keeps swinging. The S&P 500 edged down 0.1% Wednesday and could be heading for another day of modest moves following a...
